title: Reading: The Official Notice
learningGoal: Decode highly formal Sanskritized vocabulary and identify the subjunctive mood in a government announcement.
instructions: Read the following official notification from the city administration. Notice how they use 'Samasya' instead of 'Pareshani', 'Soochit' instead of 'Batana', and how the subjunctive is used after 'Taaki' and 'Chahiye ki'.
readingText:
सभी नागरिकों को सूचित किया जाता है कि जल आपूर्ति में एक समस्या आ गई है।
इस समस्या के समाधान हेतु, कल सुबह पानी उपलब्ध नहीं होगा।
अतः आपसे अनुरोध है कि आप आज ही पानी भर लें ताकि कल आपको कोई कष्ट न हो।
प्रशासन को चाहिए कि वह जल्द से जल्द मरम्मत का कार्य समाप्त करे।
असुविधा के लिए हमें खेद है। धन्यवाद।
textInTargetLanguage:
सभी नागरिकों को सूचित किया जाता है कि जल आपूर्ति में एक समस्या आ गई है।
इस समस्या के समाधान हेतु, कल सुबह पानी उपलब्ध नहीं होगा।
अतः आपसे अनुरोध है कि आप आज ही पानी भर लें ताकि कल आपको कोई कष्ट न हो।
प्रशासन को चाहिए कि वह जल्द से जल्द मरम्मत का कार्य समाप्त करे।
असुविधा के लिए हमें खेद है। धन्यवाद।
baseLanguageSupportText:
All citizens are hereby informed that a problem has occurred in the water supply.
For the purpose of the resolution of this problem, water will not be available tomorrow morning.
Therefore, it is requested of you that you fill water today itself so that tomorrow you may not have any hardship.
The administration should (it is needed to the admin that they) conclude the repair work as soon as possible.
We regret the inconvenience. Thank you.

exampleSentence.targetText: आपको सूचित किया जाता है कि...
exampleSentence.transliteration: Aapko soochit kiya jaata hai ki...

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: The resolution of the problem is necessary.

glossary.baseTranslation: for the purpose of (Highly formal instead of 'ke liye')

exampleSentence.targetText: जल्दी जाओ ताकि ट्रेन मिल जाए।
exampleSentence.transliteration: Jaldi jao taaki train mil jaaye.
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: Go fast so that you may get the train.

exampleSentence.targetText: देरी की असुविधा के लिए खेद है।
exampleSentence.transliteration: Deri ki asuvidha ke liye khed hai.
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: We regret the inconvenience of the delay.

sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: For the purpose of the resolution of this problem...
Therefore, it is requested of you that...

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: प्रशासन को चाहिए कि वह जल्द से जल्द मरम्मत का कार्य समाप्त करे।
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Prashasan ko chahiye ki woh jald se jald marammat ka karya samapt kare.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: The administration should conclude the repair work as soon as possible.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What does the phrase 'Soochit kiya jaata hai' represent?
options: A formal passive voice meaning 'It is being informed'.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Instead of using 'Pareshani ke liye' (for the problem), what highly formal word do they use for 'for the purpose of'?
Look at the sentence: '...taaki kal aapko koi kasht na ho.' Why is the verb 'ho' instead of 'hoga'?
options: Because it follows 'Taaki' (so that), which requires the subjunctive mood.
Because it is in the past tense.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: How does the notice say 'The administration should conclude...'?
options: Prashasan ko chahiye ki woh... samapt kare (Administration needs that they conclude - Subjunctive).

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What does 'Asuvidha ke liye khed hai' mean?

correctAnswers.rq_1: A formal passive voice meaning 'It is being informed'.
correctAnswers.rq_3: Because it follows 'Taaki' (so that), which requires the subjunctive mood.
correctAnswers.rq_4: Prashasan ko chahiye ki woh... samapt kare (Administration needs that they conclude - Subjunctive).

expectedSummaryPoints: Identify 'hetu' as a formal replacement for 'ke liye'.
Identify subjunctive mood after 'taaki' and 'chahiye ki'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Phenomenal! You can now decipher the complex Sanskritized language used in Indian bureaucracy.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the glossary. Words like 'Samasya', 'Anurodh', and 'Samapt' are the foundation of formal Hindi.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good progress. Pay close attention to question 4. Formal Hindi rarely uses 'You should do X'. Instead, it says 'It is needed that you do X' (Chahiye ki).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: सभी नागरिकों को सूचित किया जाता है कि जल आपूर्ति में एक समस्या आ गई है। इस समस्या के समाधान हेतु, कल सुबह पानी उपलब्ध नहीं होगा। अतः आपसे अनुरोध है कि आप आज ही पानी भर लें ताकि कल आपको कोई कष्ट न हो। प्रशासन को चाहिए कि वह जल्द से जल्द मरम्मत का कार्य समाप्त करे। असुविधा के लिए हमें खेद है। धन्यवाद।

hintText: Notice how the future 'karega' becomes 'kare' (subjunctive) when it follows 'chahiye ki' (it is needed that).
